# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/std@0.140.0/flags/mod.ts";
import { Kv } from "https://deno.land/x/denokv@1.2.0/mod.ts";

const kv = await openKv();

async function searchUsers(req: Request) {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(req.url.split("?")[1]);
  const filterParam = params.get("filter");
  const userId = params.get("userId");

  if (!userId || !filterParam) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing required parameters" }), { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const key = ["users", userId, filterParam].join("-");
    const user = await kv.get(key);
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(user), { status: 200 });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Error searching users" }), { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function main() {
  const req = await fetch(new URL("/api/search", Deno.env.get("DENON_URL") || "http://localhost:8000"));
  const res = await searchUsers(req);
  console.log(res.body);
}

main();
